cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is asking what can be inferred from the passage. The four options are (A) through (D). I'll go through each option and see if it can be inferred from the passage. Option A: "there are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar". The passage mentions that there is an inscription on the edge of the new Presidential $1 coins, which is different from the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. It says, "The Presidential $1 coins will be the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. However, there is an important difference. For the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each coin." This suggests that the Sacagawea Golden Dollar does not have an inscription on the edge, but it doesn't necessarily mean there are no words at all on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The passage only makes a comparison about the inscription on the edge, not about words elsewhere on the coin. So, this inference may not be fully supported by the passage.
